quick_facts.label: Award
quick_facts.value: At IITs, a B.Tech.–M.Tech dual-degree student is awarded both the bachelor’s and master’s degrees after completing the programme’s curriculum requirements.

quick_facts.label: Typical minimum duration
quick_facts.value: The JEE (Advanced) 2026 information brochure lists the B.Tech.–M.Tech Dual Degree with a minimum duration of five years.

quick_facts.label: IIT admission route
quick_facts.value: For IIT undergraduate dual-degree offerings, admission is through JEE (Advanced) and the applicable seat-allocation process; the exact programme list is announced for the relevant admission year and is not identical across IITs.

quick_facts.label: Programme naming and route vary
quick_facts.value: A five-year engineering bachelor–master pathway may be titled “Dual Degree B.Tech.–M.Tech.” or “Integrated M.Tech.” depending on the institution. VIT’s published Integrated M.Tech. admissions page states that its listed programmes have no entrance examination and use qualifying-examination (+2) performance, so VITEEE should not be treated as a universal admission requirement for this node.

quick_facts.label: Curriculum model
quick_facts.value: IIT undergraduate programmes are credit-based. The academic mix, specialisation, project/thesis requirements, and any minor or interdisciplinary option are determined by the individual institute and programme.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: Eligibility is route- and institution-specific. For IIT admission, candidates must satisfy the annual JEE (Advanced) eligibility rules and the Class XII/equivalent performance criterion specified in that year’s brochure; seat allocation then follows merit, category and other applicable rules. These requirements can change between admission cycles, so applicants should use the current official brochure rather than relying on prior-year cut-offs or rules. Source: https://jeeadv.ac.in/documents/IBEnglish_2026.pdf

VIT’s published page for its five-year Integrated M.Tech. programmes states a separate standard: regular, full-time and formal education; Resident Indian nationality; admission based on qualifying-examination (+2) performance; and PCM aggregate thresholds of 70% for Vellore/Chennai and 65% for VIT-AP/VIT Bhopal, with stated relaxations for specified groups. Applicants should verify the live campus-specific notice before applying. Source: https://stage.vit.ac.in/all-courses/ug/integrated-m.tech-programmes
bullets: For IIT routes, confirm JEE (Advanced) eligibility, Class XII/equivalent requirements, programme availability and JoSAA choices for the intended academic year.
For private/university integrated routes, check the institution’s own +2 subjects, marks, nationality/category and application requirements.

WBJEE is the West Bengal board’s entrance examination for undergraduate Engineering & Technology, Pharmacy and Architecture admissions in participating West Bengal institutions. It should only be treated as a route to this exact dual-degree credential when the current participating-institute seat matrix explicitly lists the programme. Source: https://wbjeeb.nic.in/wbjee/

sections.heading: Duration and qualification outcome
paragraphs: The standard IIT B.Tech.–M.Tech dual degree is a five-year programme. The JEE (Advanced) 2026 brochure distinguishes it from a four-year B.Tech. and states that both bachelor’s and master’s degrees are awarded when the dual-degree curriculum requirements are successfully completed. Source: https://jeeadv.ac.in/documents/IBEnglish_2026.pdf

The exact label matters: some institutions advertise a five-year “Integrated M.Tech.” rather than a separately named B.Tech.–M.Tech dual degree. For example, VIT lists several five-year Integrated M.Tech. programmes. Applicants should confirm the precise degree title printed in the institute’s prospectus and award regulations. Source: https://stage.vit.ac.in/all-courses/ug/integrated-m.tech-programmes
bullets: Minimum duration at IITs: five years.
Outcome at IIT dual-degree programmes: bachelor’s and master’s awards upon successful completion.
Availability is not universal: the JEE brochure states that not every listed programme/course is offered at every IIT, and the current-year list is announced during seat allocation.

sections.heading: Curriculum and academic structure
paragraphs: The curriculum is not nationally uniform. IITs describe these undergraduate programmes as credit-based, with institute-specific academic-performance requirements for options such as minors and, in some cases, interdisciplinary dual degrees. The branch, M.Tech. specialisation, credit distribution, laboratory work and final project/thesis rules must therefore be checked in the institute’s current curriculum. Source: https://jeeadv.ac.in/documents/IBEnglish_2026.pdf

As an illustrative official curriculum—not a universal template—IIT Kharagpur’s Mechanical Engineering dual-degree curriculum includes foundational subjects such as mathematics, physics, chemistry, programming and data structures, electrical technology, engineering drawing, manufacturing processes and associated laboratories in the early semesters. Later coursework and the master’s component are branch- and institute-specific. Source: https://erp.iitkgp.ac.in/ERPWebServices/curricula/CurriculaSubjectsList.jsp?splCode=MED&stuType=UG
bullets: Expect foundational engineering and science study early in the programme, followed by branch/specialisation coursework; verify this against the chosen institute’s curriculum.
A dual-degree student may be able to pursue a minor at some IITs; any such option is subject to institute-prescribed academic-performance criteria.
Do not assume that every B.Tech.–M.Tech programme has the same specialisation pairing or thesis/project structure.

sections.heading: Admission routes and application strategy
paragraphs: For IIT entry, JEE (Advanced) is the principal route for the B.Tech.–M.Tech dual degrees covered by the JEE brochure. Candidates who meet the examination and Class XII/equivalent conditions participate in the relevant seat-allocation process; programme availability for 2026–27 is to be announced by JoSAA during choice filling. Source: https://jeeadv.ac.in/documents/IBEnglish_2026.pdf

The node’s named entrance examinations are not interchangeable pathways. VIT currently lists a separate five-year Integrated M.Tech. admission process with no entrance examination and selection based on +2 performance, whereas WBJEE conducts an undergraduate entrance examination for participating West Bengal institutions. Before treating VITEEE or WBJEE as a route to this credential, verify that the current institution and seat matrix specifically offer the five-year programme. Sources: https://stage.vit.ac.in/all-courses/ug/integrated-m.tech-programmes ; https://wbjeeb.nic.in/wbjee/
bullets: IIT: track the annual JEE (Advanced) brochure, result/rank process and JoSAA programme list.
VIT Integrated M.Tech.: use VIT’s dedicated integrated-programme admission notice, not assumptions based on VITEEE.
WBJEE: inspect the current counselling/participating-institute material for a specifically listed dual or integrated B.Tech.–M.Tech programme.
Treat application dates, seat matrices, eligibility percentages and reservation/document rules as year-specific.

sections.heading: Options after or during the programme
paragraphs: Students in, or graduates of, a five-year engineering dual/integrated master’s programme can be eligible to take GATE under the published GATE 2026 rules: candidates in the third, fourth or fifth year of a five-year integrated master’s or dual-degree engineering/technology programme, or those who have completed it, are listed as eligible. GATE qualification does not by itself guarantee admission, employment or funding; the receiving institution or employer sets its own criteria. Source: https://gate2026.iitg.ac.in/doc/IB/GATE2026-IB-v22082025-1300.pdf

Doctoral study is also a possible route, subject to each institute’s current criteria. As dated institutional context, IIT Delhi’s 2025–26 prospectus lists a relevant master’s degree in Engineering/Technology with the stated minimum academic threshold among its full-time Ph.D. eligibility routes; future cycles may use different department-level shortlisting and test/interview requirements. Source: https://home.iitd.ac.in/uploads/prospectus/IITD%20Prospecus%202025-26.pdf
bullets: Use the combined engineering and master’s training for technical roles, research-oriented roles or further study; employers set role-specific hiring requirements.
Consider GATE where it fits the intended postgraduate, public-sector or other opportunity pathway; check the applicable year’s brochure and the receiving organisation’s rules.
For Ph.D. applications, verify the target department’s discipline match, academic threshold, test/fellowship requirements and current selection process.
